title: Xenial Tunisian Onion Tagine
description: A buttery, molten stew of caramelized yellow onion, infused with cumin, coriander, smoked paprika, and a splash of lemon, served steaming in a tagine for a festive flexitarian delight.
instructions: Heat a heavy‑bottomed pot over medium heat and melt the butter until it foams.
Add the sliced onion, a pinch of salt, and the minced garlic, and cook, stirring, until the onion becomes translucent and begins to brown, about 10 minutes.
Stir in the cumin, coriander, smoked paprika, black pepper, and lemon zest, and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in the vegetable broth, bringing the mixture to a gentle simmer, and let it reduce for 10 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld.
Stir in the lemon juice, and simmer for another 5 minutes, then remove the pot from heat.
Transfer the hot mixture into a ceramic or glass tagine, or a shallow oven‑proof dish.
Sprinkle the chopped parsley over the top, cover, and bake at 180°C for 20 minutes to steam the onion into a creamy, buttery sauce.
Remove the lid and bake uncovered for an additional 5 minutes to thicken the glaze to a molten consistency.
Let the tagine rest for 5 minutes before serving, allowing the heat to distribute evenly.
Garnish with extra parsley or a drizzle of olive oil if desired, and serve hot with crusty bread.
